task_completion.description: Record the specific organophosphate toxic effect diagnosis
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a diagnosis, condition, or pathology record in GNU Health for patient John Zenon with an ICD-10 code starting with 'T60' (e.g., T60.0, Toxic effect of pesticides).

task_completion.description: Perform a clinical evaluation documenting bradycardia
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a clinical evaluation, encounter, or vital signs record in GNU Health for John Zenon showing a heart rate (HR or bpm) value of 60 or less.
Look for a prescription or medication order in GNU Health for John Zenon where the medication name includes 'Atropine'.

task_completion.description: Order at least two toxicity monitoring labs
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for lab test requests or orders in GNU Health for John Zenon showing at least two distinct lab tests being ordered (e.g., cholinesterase levels, basic metabolic panel).

task_completion.description: Schedule a follow-up appointment within 1 to 5 days
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an appointment record or scheduling screen in GNU Health for John Zenon with an appointment date set 1 to 5 days in the future.

integrity.description: Agent used the GNU Health graphical interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify the agent is interacting with the GNU Health desktop client windows and forms, rather than using a terminal to execute commands.

integrity.description: Agent did not use direct database insertion
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal windows open showing SQL queries, sqlite3, or psql commands used to insert data directly into the backend database.

integrity.description: Agent did not spoof answers in a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify the clinical data is entered into actual GNU Health application fields and not just written out in a text editor, notepad, or word processor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Patient: John Zenon. Diagnosis must use an ICD-10 code starting with 'T60' (organophosphate poisoning). Clinical evaluation must document a heart rate of 60 or lower. Medication prescribed must be 'Atropine'. At least 2 lab orders must be placed. A follow-up appointment must be scheduled 1 to 5 days from the current date.
